perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby agree that the statement on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the undersigned shall be, filed on behalf of each of the undersigned pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.changed_text: The filers hereby acknowledge that the report on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the filers shall be, submitted on behalf of each of the filers pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'undersigned' and 'filers' are used interchangeably in the first sentence. Later in the document, only 'undersigned' is used. This creates confusion about whether all subsequent obligations apply equally to entities defined as 'filers' in this initial statement. It creates uncertainty on the scope of which entities are part of this agreement, and can lead to potential disagreement on which 'filers' are bound by the agreement terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The filers hereby acknowledge that the report on Schedule 13G with respect to the equity securities of Q Biomed, Inc. is, and any amendment thereto signed by each of the filers shall be, submitted on behalf of each of the filers pursuant to and in accordance with the provisions of Rule 13d-1(k) promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended. The undersigned hereby further agree that this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'undersigned,' and the changed text introduces 'filers' interchangeably with 'undersigned' in the first sentence. The contradicted text only uses 'undersigned,' creating ambiguity as to whether the obligations in the contradicted text apply to all 'filers' or just the 'undersigned.' This misalignment of terminology directly creates a conflict, as it is unclear who is bound by the Joint Filing Agreement referenced in the contradicted text, fitting the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned hereby further agree that this Joint Filing Agreement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned hereby further stipulate that this Filing Arrangement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'Joint Filing Agreement' and 'Filing Arrangement' are used interchangeably. This can cause confusion since it wasn't defined that both terms represent the same, creating uncertainty if this document covers the conditions of a 'Filing Arrangement' or 'Joint Filing Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned hereby further stipulate that this Filing Arrangement be included as an exhibit to such statement and any such amendment. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Joint Filing Agreement,' while the changed text uses 'Filing Arrangement.' The Perturbation Explanation states that these terms are used interchangeably, but not explicitly defined as such, creating uncertainty. The Contradicted Text uses both terms. This interchangeable, yet undefined, use creates a contradiction because it's unclear if the obligations and conditions apply to both a 'Filing Arrangement' and a 'Joint Filing Agreement,' or if they are distinct, impacting enforcement. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each bears liability for the timely submission of such changes, and for the integrity and precision of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but will not be held liable for the integrity and precision of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces several key terms: 'responsible for the timely filing' becomes 'bears liability for the timely submission,' and 'completeness and accuracy' becomes 'integrity and precision.' The document does not clarify that these different terms mean the same thing, this difference might create doubt on which obligation the parties signed for. The degree of 'integrity and precision' is a matter of interpretation, which may not always align with 'completeness and accuracy', or it can lead to disagreements over the level of due diligence required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each bears liability for the timely submission of such changes, and for the integrity and precision of the information concerning him or it contained herein and therein, but will not be held liable for the integrity and precision of the information concerning the others. This Joint Filing Agreement may be executed in any number of counterparts, all of which taken together shall constitute one and the same instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'responsible for the timely filing' with 'bears liability for the timely submission,' and 'completeness and accuracy' with 'integrity and precision.' While the explanation suggests this creates ambiguity, both clauses are present in the 'Contradicted Text,' rendering them non-contradictory. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed Text, so they do not contradict each other. The replaced terms could potentially be interpreted differently, but the fact that the Changed Text and Contradicted Text are the same indicates there is no strong contradiction.
